title: galium rotundifolium subsp. ellipticum
intro: Galium rotundifolium subsp. ellipticum, also known as elliptical-leaved bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium rotundifolium plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the madder family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including wound healing and the reduction of inflammation. The herbal preparations of Galium rotundifolium subsp. ellipticum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and upset stomachs, due to its anti-diarrheal properties.
